name: neosctl
documentation:
# Core CLI v0.12.31

## Setup

### Install CLI

Install the CLI using [pip](https://pypi.org/project/neosctl/):

```bash
pip install neosctl
```

See [Local Development](https://github.com/NEOS-Critical/neos-platform-cli/tree/main/DEVELOP.md) for details on installing from source.

### Setup environment

To setup an environment, run the following command:

```bash
neosctl env init <env-name> -h <hub-host> -u <username> -a <account>
```
More information about this command you can find in the [DOCS.md](https://github.com/NEOS-Critical/neos-platform-cli/tree/main/DOCS.md) file.

### Activate an environment
To activate an environment (for use in subsequent requests):

```bash
neosctl env activate <env-name>
```

### Login to the system

To login to the system, run the following command:

```bash
neosctl env login
```

You will need username and password for that.

### Activate a core
To activate a core (for use in subsequent requests):

```bash
neoctl env list-cores
neosctl env activate-core <core-name>
```

### Setup service user (optional)

For some operations, you will need to provide a service user `access_key_id`
and `secret_access_key`. To create service user and get it's access and secret
key, use:

```bash
neosctl env whoami

{
  "user_id": "<user-id>"
}
```

```bash
neosctl iam user create-access-key <user-id>
```

To configure the environment to use the key pair:

```bash
neosctl env credential <env-name> <access-key> <secret-key>
```

### Review settings

All setting are stored by default in the folder `~/.neosctl/`.

You can also review all settings by running the following commands:

```bash
neosctl env list
neosctl env view <env-name>
neosctl env active
```

## Usage

To see all available commands, run the following command:

```bash
neosctl --help
```

or go to the [DOCS.md](https://github.com/NEOS-Critical/neos-platform-cli/tree/main/DOCS.md) file.

modules.name: neosctl.http
documentation: HTTP Client implementation for calling NEOS services.

functions.name: neosctl.http.log_request
documentation: Event hook for httpx events to log requests.

classes.name: neosctl.http.RequestException
documentation: Base class for all http request failures.

classes.name: neosctl.http.NeosBearerClientAuth
documentation: Bearer token auth implementation for httpx.

classes.name: neosctl.http.NeosClient
documentation: Generic HTTP client supporting Bearer and NEOS-HMAC-SHA256 auth.

modules.name: neosctl.profile
documentation: Profile commands, like profile initialisation, deletion, and listing.

functions.name: neosctl.profile._validate_url
documentation: Validate a given url is a valid url with schema.

functions.name: neosctl.profile.init
documentation:
Initialise a profile.

Create a profile that can be reused in later commands to define which
services to interact with, and which user to interact as.

Call `init` on an existing profile will update the existing profile.

modules.name: neosctl.env
documentation: Environment commands, like env initialisation, deletion, and listing.

functions.name: neosctl.env._validate_url
documentation: Validate a given url is a valid url with schema.

functions.name: neosctl.env.get_cores
documentation: Get list of cores from registry.

functions.name: neosctl.env.init
documentation:
Initialise an environment.

Create an environment that can be reused in later commands to define which
services to interact with, and which user to interact as.

Call `init` on an existing environment will update it.

functions.name: neosctl.env.activate
documentation:
Activate an environment.

Activate an environment to use its configuration for subsequent requests.

functions.name: neosctl.env.credentials
documentation: Configure access keys for an environment.

functions.name: neosctl.env.activate_core
documentation: Activate a core in current environment.

methods.name: neosctl.schema.Core.convert_null_account
documentation: Toml treats None as "null", convert back to None.

methods.name: neosctl.schema.Env.convert_null_account
documentation: Toml treats None as "null", convert back to None.

methods.name: neosctl.schema.Common.gateway_api_url
documentation:
Return gateway api url.

If a user profile is provided and defines a gateway url, return that,
otherwise or fall back to cli defined default.

methods.name: neosctl.schema.Common.storage_api_url
documentation:
Return storage api url.

If a user profile is provided and defines a storage url, return that,
otherwise or fall back to cli defined default.

methods.name: neosctl.schema.Common.hub_api_url
documentation:
Return hub api url.

If a user profile is provided and defines a hub url, return that,
otherwise fall back to cli defined default.

functions.name: neosctl.cli._generate_common_schema
documentation:
Generate a common schema object.

Set the default api values using user_profile and cli provided defaults.

functions.name: neosctl.cli._callback
documentation:
Inject common configuration defaults into context.

Allow missing user_profile to support profile generation etc.

functions.name: neosctl.services.registry.registry.register_core
documentation:
Register a core.

Register a core to receive an identifier and access key for use in deployment.

functions.name: neosctl.services.registry.registry.migrate_core
documentation:
Migrate a core out of root account.

Migrate a core from `root` into an actual account.

functions.name: neosctl.services.storage.storage.set_object_tags

Dump formatted json.

json.dump provided payload with indent 2 and (un)sorted keys.

functions.name: neosctl.util.read_config_dotfile
documentation: Read in `.neosctl/profile` configuration file and parse.

functions.name: neosctl.util.read_env_dotfile
documentation: Read in `.neosctl/env` configuration file and parse.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_optional_user_profile_section
documentation:
Get profile from neosctl configuration.

Returns:
-------
Return configparser.SectionProxy if found, or None.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_user_profile_section
documentation:
Get profile from neosctl configuration.

If profile is not found exit cli.

Returns:
-------
configparser.SectionProxy for the requested profile.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_env_section
documentation:
Get env from neosctl configuration.

If env is not found exit cli.

Returns:
-------
schema.Env for the requested environment.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_user_credential
documentation:
Get profile from neosctl credentials.

If profile is not found exit cli.

Returns:
-------
schema.Credential for the requested profile.

functions.name: neosctl.util.ensure_login
documentation:
Capture authentication errors and retry requests.

On request failure check if the response is a 401, and attempt to refresh the access_token.
Retry the request with refreshed token, on subsequent failure, return.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_user_profile
documentation:
Get user profile.

If allow_missing if False and the profile is not found, the cli will exit with an error output.

functions.name: neosctl.util.check_profile_exists
documentation: Check if a profile (or env) exists in neosctl configuration or exit.

functions.name: neosctl.util.check_env_active
documentation: Check if a profile (or env) exists in neosctl configuration or exit.

functions.name: neosctl.util.upsert_config
documentation: Update neosctl configuration profile in place.

functions.name: neosctl.util.upsert_env
documentation: Update neosctl env configuration in place.

functions.name: neosctl.util.upsert_credential
documentation: Update neosctl credential profile in place.

functions.name: neosctl.util.remove_config
documentation: Remove a profile from neosctl configuration.

functions.name: neosctl.util.remove_env
documentation: Remove an environment from neosctl configuration.

functions.name: neosctl.util.get_file_location
documentation: Get a Path for the provided filepath, exit if not found.

documentation: Load contents of json file, exit if not found.

functions.name: neosctl.util.load_object
documentation: Trying to read and parse JSON file into the given data type.

functions.name: neosctl.util.extract_account
documentation: Set up account header and params.
